abstract.t: This document defines the Deferred Token Response (DTR) extension for OAuth 2.1. In existing OAuth grants, the token endpoint either issues an access token or returns an error. DTR establishes a generic asynchronous token request mechanism that any OAuth grant may plug into. In DTR-aware flows, the authorization server returns a deferral_code and a polling interval, indicating that the final token response will be available at a later time. The client retrieves the eventual response by polling the token endpoint, or by receiving a callback from the authorization server when one is configured.
